key_insight: LapidaryEngine enables fully conversational crystal design by translating free-form language into crystal structures and back through a pivot representation for iterative refinement.
engineering_relevance: This could let materials engineers specify target properties in natural language, then interactively edit candidate crystals for faster discovery and tighter intent alignment.
method: Accepts free-form natural-language requests instead of only structured formulas
Uses a pivot representation as an intermediate bridge for bidirectional text↔crystal translation
Supports dialogue-like iterative refinement, editing, and feedback-driven updates
evidence_signal: The abstract reports demonstrations across insulator discovery, stability optimization, compositional modification, and structural editing, showing task coverage rather than a single static generation benchmark.
watch_item: Performance may depend on the quality of the pivot representation and robustness to ambiguous user prompts, especially without direct paired text-crystal data.
